content.content:
1	# Data Description
2	
3	> **Path note (2026-04-28):** `notebooks/`, `sessions/`, and
4	> `full_automatic_execution_01/` were moved to
5	> `/root/capsule/data/claude_data/` (read-only). Any reference to
6	> `code/notebooks/...`, `code/sessions/...`, or
7	> `code/full_automatic_execution_01/...` below resolves to
8	> `data/claude_data/<same suffix>`. New writes must live under
9	> `/root/capsule/code/` (e.g.,
10	> `code/full_automatic_execution_02/sessions/...`).
11	
12	This document describes the two imaging modalities and the properties of the
13	coregistration problem, with concrete numbers measured from the 6 benchmark
14	subjects (755252, 767018, 767022, 782149, 788406, 790322). Numbers come from
15	the analysis in `code/notebooks/01_benchmark_data_analysis.ipynb`
16	and `code/sessions/01_analyze_benchmark_data/`.
17	
18	## Modalities
19	
20	### 1) 2p GCaMP (Source Sub-volume)
21	- In-vivo 2-photon cortical z-stack (CZStack).
22	- Image size: 512 × 512 × ~450 planes.
23	- Field of view options (from `manual workflow/step_1_process_files.ipynb`):
24	  400 um (XY resolution 0.78 um/pixel) or 700 um (1.37 um/pixel). All current
25	  benchmark subjects use 400 um.
26	- Z resolution: ~1 um/pixel.
27	- Physical extent (measured from centroids, per subject): CZ cells span
28	  ~392–398 um (x), ~392–396 um (y), ~404–424 um (z).
29	- Content: ~785–1016 GCaMP+ inhibitory neurons, with 3D ROI segmentation masks.
30	- From pia surface; the top ~50–110 um is blank buffer (no cells). Measured
31	  pia-plane offsets per subject: 55–108 um.
32	- Sub-volume embedded within the larger HCR volume.
33	
34	### 2) HCR Light-sheet (Target Volume)
35	- Ex-vivo HCR light-sheet imaging, matched to the in-vivo 2p volume (but
36	  larger).
37	- Raw XY resolution ≈ 0.245 um/pixel; Z resolution ≈ 1 um/pixel.
38	- **Cell centroids are stored at level-2 pyramid pixels** (`cell_centroids.npy`),
39	  where XY has been downsampled 4× from raw. This is the coordinate system the
40	  manual coregistration workflow operates in, using the conversion from
41	  `manual workflow/step_2_automatic_mapping_for_qc.ipynb`:
42	
43	  ```
44	  scale_x = dimensions.x[0] * 4e6   # ~0.98 um per level-2 pixel
45	  scale_y = dimensions.y[0] * 4e6
46	  scale_z = dimensions.z[0] * 1e6   # ~1 um per pixel
47	  ```
48	
49	- Level-2 effective resolution: ~0.980–0.989 um/pixel XY, ~1 um/pixel Z
50	  (slightly varies per subject; essentially isotropic in pixel space).
51	- Physical extent (level-2 centroids): ~1.8–2.3 mm XY, 0.88–1.34 mm Z.
52	  (The earlier "~1 × 1 × 0.5 mm" figure was based on raw-pixel volume, not the
53	  centroid coordinate space. The coregistration works in the centroid space.)
54	- Content per volume: ~39 k–127 k Rn28S-segmented cells (405 nm); 488-channel
55	  GFP+ subset ≈ 25 k–78 k at a 5-spot-per-cell threshold (matching the 2p
56	  GCaMP+ neurons).
57	- 3D ROI segmentation from Rn28S; 488-channel GFP quantification comes from
58	  one of three sources (same convention as
59	  `manual workflow/step_2_automatic_mapping_for_qc.ipynb`):
60	  1. pre-aggregated per-cell counts
61	     `{coreg_dir}/*_spot_488_counts.csv` (782149, 788406, 790322).
62	  2. raw per-spot CSV aggregated on the fly via `SEG_ID.value_counts()` and
63	     joined with `cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle` for volume/density
64	     (767018 and any subject that lacks the pre-aggregated file).
65	  3. per-cell mean intensity `data/cell_data_mean_{subj}_R1.csv` channel 488
66	     (755252, 767022). No spot count is available here, only a fluorescence
67	     intensity.
68	  A GFP+ cell is defined by `counts >= gfp_min_spots` (default 5) when a spot
69	  count exists. The loader exposes `gfp_min_spots` as a parameter so the
70	  threshold can be revised from later analyses. Intensity-only subjects use
71	  the raw `mean` and must be thresholded by the caller.
72	- Top part contains blank buffer. Thickness is unknown, varies across subjects
73	  (measured pia offsets: 30–410 um), and is **slanted** (measured pia-plane
74	  tilt: 1.2°–11.6°, with 782149 the most tilted).
75	
76	## Benchmark-subject statistics
77	
78	Measured per-subject values (all coordinates converted to physical microns).
79	
80	GFP+ counts below use the default 5-spot threshold where applicable.
81	
82	| subject | CZ cells | HCR total | HCR GFP+ (source, threshold)          | coreg matched | match rate |
83	|---------|---------:|----------:|---------------------------------------|--------------:|-----------:|
84	| 755252  | 835      | 84 233    | 77 785 (intensity_r1, no threshold)   | 639           | 76.5%      |
85	| 767018  | 785      | 108 506   | 58 959 (aggregated_spots_csv, ≥5)     | 273           | 34.8%      |
86	| 767022  | 926      | 76 336    | 72 213 (intensity_r1, no threshold)   | 793           | 85.6%      |
87	| 782149  | 894      | 39 291    | 25 251 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 303           | 33.9%      |
88	| 788406  | 932      | 127 275   | 69 680 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 787           | 84.4%      |
89	| 790322  | 1016     | 106 379   | 72 532 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 778           | 76.6%      |
90	
91	Notes:
92	- 767018 lacks the pre-aggregated `spot_488_counts.csv` but has the raw
93	  `image_spot_detection/channel_488_spots/spots.csv` (4.86 M puncta). The
94	  loader aggregates it per `SEG_ID` at load time. At a 5-spot threshold,
95	  76 607 cells with ≥1 spot reduce to 58 959 GFP+.
96	- For 755252/767022 the "GFP+" figure is every HCR cell with a non-zero 488
97	  intensity measurement (no spot threshold available). A downstream intensity
98	  threshold is required to obtain a comparable GFP+ count.
99	
100	## Key relationship between 2p and HCR
101	
102	- CZ sub-volume is localized inside HCR.
103	- **XY rotation is ~180°** around the z-axis (measured: angles −177° to +180°,
104	  always near 180° in absolute value; this is not a flip).
105	- Both start from the cortex surface (pia at top, deep cortex at bottom).
106	- ROI segmentations have errors in both modalities, so ROI-to-ROI is not 1:1.
107	
108	## Transform characterisation
109	
110	Measured from active landmarks (500–800 per subject, final QC'd iteration).
111	An anisotropic similarity is fit (rotation + diagonal scale + translation).
112	
113	### Anisotropic expansion (CZ in-vivo → HCR ex-vivo, physical microns)
114	
115	| subject | XY expansion (mean) | Z expansion | XY:Z anisotropy |
116	|---------|--------------------:|------------:|----------------:|
117	| 755252  | 1.64×               | 2.13×       | 0.77            |
118	| 767018  | 1.70×               | 3.58×       | 0.47            |
119	| 767022  | 1.81×               | 2.49×       | 0.73            |
120	| 782149  | 1.92×               | 2.93×       | 0.66            |
121	| 788406  | 1.78×               | 2.82×       | 0.63            |
122	| 790322  | 1.77×               | 3.04×       | 0.58            |
123	
124	Expansion is consistently anisotropic: XY expands ~1.6–1.9×, Z expands ~2.1–3.6×
125	(Z is ~1.5× more expanded than XY). A single isotropic scale cannot align the
126	modalities.
127	
128	### Residual deformation after anisotropic affine (landmark RMS, microns)
129	
130	| subject | residual RMS | residual max |
131	|---------|-------------:|-------------:|
132	| 755252  | 27           | 83           |
133	| 767018  | 25           | 88           |
134	| 767022  | 16           | 53           |
135	| 782149  | 29           | 65           |
136	| 788406  | 43           | 114          |
137	| 790322  | 22           | 91           |
138	
139	After the affine fit, residual magnitudes reach ~25–45 um RMS, up to ~110 um
140	max. This is the scale of the nonrigid deformation the algorithm must handle
141	after affine alignment.
142	
143	### Anisotropy introduced by coordinate systems
144	
145	In addition to the physical expansion, the pixel coordinate systems themselves
146	are slightly anisotropic: CZ is 0.78 um/px XY vs 1 um/px Z, and HCR is ~0.988
147	um/px XY vs 1 um/px Z. When registering in pixel space, the RBF absorbs both
148	the physical expansion and this pixel-space anisotropy.
149	
150	## Pia surface and depth-from-surface coordinate
151	
152	Two surface-fitting methods were evaluated:
153	
154	1. **Image-based (primary)**: for each (y, x) column of the 3D image, find
155	   the first z at which intensity exceeds a per-column relative threshold
156	   `col_bg + 0.05 × (col_top − col_bg)` (`col_bg = 10-pct`,
157	   `col_top = 95-pct`). The low 5 % margin is essential — larger margins
158	   (25 % initially tried) skip the sparse outer tissue layer and anchor the
159	   plane in dense cortex. Require a ≥ 15 um contiguous above-threshold
160	   segment and reject columns whose dynamic range is below a minimum.
161	   IRLS-Huber plane fit with tile-median outlier rejection.
162	
163	   **For HCR** we use a **combined-channel volume**: load every available
164	   channel (405, 488, 561 or 514, 594), normalise each by its own
165	   background/percentile, and sum. Autofluorescence aligns across channels
166	   exactly at the tissue–buffer transition, producing a crisper boundary
167	   than any single channel. 488 alone is too sparse (~100–200 valid columns
168	   vs ~30 000); 405 alone works but can overshoot into dense cortex.
169	   Level 4 of the zarr pyramid (~4 um / voxel) is used.
170	
171	2. **Hybrid (default for HCR)**: Uses ROI segmentation data to constrain the
172	   image-based search window. Steps:
173	   (a) Density filter: keep only ROIs with ≥6 neighbors within 30 um (removes
174	       isolated out-of-tissue false positives).
175	   (b) Fit a plane to the minimum-z ROI per (x, y) tile (coarse prior
176	       `z_prior(x, y)`).
177	   (c) In the image-based search, restrict each column to z ∈
178	       [z_prior − 100 um, z_prior + 100 um] before finding the first-crossing.
179	   (d) Fit the final plane to the constrained first-crossing map.
180	   This exploits the ROI-based coarse localization as a guard against the
181	   image-based search getting confused by noise far from the tissue boundary.
182	   **It is the current default in `analyze_subject()`.**
183	
184	3. **Centroid-based (fallback)**: tile-top quantile of ROI centroids + IRLS-
185	   Huber plane fit. Kept for diagnostic comparison. It systematically
186	   underestimates the pia by 50–200 um because the topmost segmented ROIs
187	   already sit below the real tissue boundary.
188	
189	Fit quality is judged by the **fraction-above-pia**: share of cells whose
190	centroid lies > 5 um above the estimated pia. It should be small (<~10 %);
191	residual positives are out-of-tissue segmentation false positives or a thin
192	layer of pial/meningeal cells.
193	
194	### Hybrid fits (current default for HCR)
195	
196	| subject | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
197	|---------|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
198	| 755252  | 259        | 7.8          | 37             | 4.0 %          |
199	| 767018  | 130        | 6.6          | 37             | 2.5 %          |
200	| 767022  | 267        | 3.1          | 69             | 4.4 %          |
201	| 782149  | 6          | 11.4         | 35             | 5.7 %          |
202	| 788406  | 148        | 2.9          | 57             | 5.8 %          |
203	| 790322  | 236        | 3.7          | 47             | 8.2 %          |
204	
205	Hybrid is better than image-only for 767018 (10.8% → 2.5%) and 782149
206	(8.2% → 5.7%); slightly worse for 755252 and 790322 (< 2 pp regression).
207	The constrained search eliminates the case where the image-based fitter
208	wanders into a noise peak far above the tissue.
209	
210	### Image-based fits (for comparison)
211	
212	| subject | CZ c (um) | CZ tilt (°) | CZ rough (um) | CZ frac-above | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
213	|---------|---------:|------------:|--------------:|--------------:|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
214	| 755252  | 57       | 3.1         | 12            | 1.0 %         | 202        | 9.3          | 39             | 2.1 %          |
215	| 767018  | 46       | 1.9         | 4             | 0.0 %         | 238        | 6.5          | 106            | 10.8 %         |
216	| 767022  | 52       | 0.7         | 7             | 0.3 %         | 290        | 3.9          | 87             | 4.6 %          |
217	| 782149  | 51       | 2.0         | 10            | 3.8 %         | 18         | 11.1         | 45             | 8.2 %          |
218	| 788406  | 57       | 2.9         | 4             | 0.0 %         | 135        | 2.8          | 93             | 5.2 %          |
219	| 790322  | 50       | 1.0         | 3             | 0.0 %         | 169        | 3.2          | 89             | 6.6 %          |
220	
221	### Centroid-based fits (for comparison)
222	
223	| subject | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
224	|---------|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
225	| 755252  | 390        | 6.9          | 96             | 9.0 %          |
226	| 767018  | 315        | 5.0          | 124            | 11.2 %         |
227	| 767022  | 409        | 3.0          | 84             | 8.4 %          |
228	| 782149  | 30         | 11.6         | 94             | 15.7 %         |
229	| 788406  | 248        | 1.2          | 98             | 7.6 %          |
230	| 790322  | 256        | 4.0          | 85             | 8.5 %          |
231	
232	Observations:
233	- CZ pia offset is tight (46–57 um), tilt ≤ 3°, roughness ≤ 12 um — a
234	  plane fit on the 3D image is essentially perfect.
235	- HCR pia offset varies widely (18–290 um, the "slanted" remark is
236	  reflected in tilts up to ~11°). 782149 is the thinnest section — its
237	  small offset (c=18) means the zarr starts essentially at the pia.
238	- `fraction-above-pia` is 0–11 % for image-based fits, ~8–16 % for
239	  centroid-based fits. The remaining positives are mostly out-of-tissue
240	  segmentation false positives and a few meningeal cells just above the
241	  cortex proper.
242	- Depth-from-surface (`depth = z − z_surface(x, y)`) is a robust axial
243	  coordinate that removes buffer-thickness and pia-tilt confounds.
244	
245	## Candidate features for automatic coregistration
246	
247	Measured on all 6 subjects:
248	
249	- **Cell nearest-neighbor distances** (mean 1-NN, um):
250	  - CZ: 21.5–24.6; HCR all: 17.1–21.1; HCR GFP+: 18.6–25.0.
251	  - Characterises packing density; informs k-NN radii for matching.
252	- **Depth density profiles**: cells/20-um bin vs depth from pia, for CZ, HCR
253	  total, and HCR GFP+. Cross-correlation (after Z-expansion rescaling) provides
254	  a 1D axial localization cue. Peaks/troughs in density reflect cortical layer
255	  structure and can be used as landmarks.
256	- **Pia surface normal**: an initial-alignment cue independent of cell
257	  positions; the CZ and HCR surface normals should be aligned after the ~180°
258	  rotation.
259	- **GFP+ local density peaks**: candidate constellations for the manual
260	  workflow's "unique clustered pattern" step.
261	- **ROI volumes / morphology** (from HCR `cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle`
262	  and CZ segmentation masks): available for per-cell matching confidence.
263	
264	## Coordinate-system conventions (summary)
265	
266	| source | column order | units | notes |
267	|--------|--------------|-------|-------|
268	| CZ centroid CSV | `[czstack_cell_id, czstack_z, czstack_y, czstack_x]` | pixels (0.78 um XY, 1 um Z) | |
269	| HCR centroid NPY | `[z, y, x, id]` | pixels (HCR level-2 ~0.988 um XY, 1 um Z) | |
270	| Landmark CSV | `[ids, active, cz_x, cz_y, cz_z, hcr_x, hcr_y, hcr_z]` | pixels in each modality's native space | no header |
271	| `spot_488_counts.csv` | `[hcr_id, counts, volume, global_bbox, density]` | 488 GFP feature | only for 782149/788406/790322 |
272	| `cell_data_mean_*_R1.csv` | channel-indexed intensity means | raw intensity | GFP+ proxy for 755252/767022 |
273	| Coreg table | `[czstack_id, hcr_id]` | — | final mapping |
274	
275	## Data-format variations across subjects
276	
277	- **767018**: uses an older processing pipeline. No `fused_ng.json` (use the
278	  fallback resolution). HCR cell centroids are in
279	  `767018_HCR_cell_centroids.csv` in addition to
280	  `cell_body_segmentation/cell_centroids.npy`. GFP+ is derived by aggregating
281	  the raw `image_spot_detection/channel_488_spots/spots.csv` on `SEG_ID` at
282	  load time (no pre-aggregated `spot_488_counts.csv`).
283	- **755252, 767022**: no `spot_488_counts.csv`. Use
284	  `/root/capsule/data/cell_data_mean_{subject}_R1.csv` with channel 488 mean
285	  intensity as GFP+ proxy. Thresholding is required to obtain a true GFP+ set.
286	- **782149**: thinner HCR section (878 um Z vs 1160–1329 um for others);
287	  strongest pia tilt (~12°); lowest match rate (~34%).
288	- **Landmark file naming**: older subjects (755252, 767018, 767022) use a
289	  `_reordered_qced` suffix; newer ones use `_qced`. Benchmark loaders must
290	  handle both.
291	
292	## Testing priorities
293	
294	- Primary: **788406** and **790322** (highest-quality data, spot-based GFP+).
295	- Secondary: **767018** (older pipeline) and **782149** (thin section, tilted
296	  pia).

# Data Description

> **Path note (2026-04-28):** `notebooks/`, `sessions/`, and
> `full_automatic_execution_01/` were moved to
> `/root/capsule/data/claude_data/` (read-only). Any reference to
> `code/notebooks/...`, `code/sessions/...`, or
> `code/full_automatic_execution_01/...` below resolves to
> `data/claude_data/<same suffix>`. New writes must live under
> `/root/capsule/code/` (e.g.,
> `code/full_automatic_execution_02/sessions/...`).

This document describes the two imaging modalities and the properties of the
coregistration problem, with concrete numbers measured from the 6 benchmark
subjects (755252, 767018, 767022, 782149, 788406, 790322). Numbers come from
the analysis in `code/notebooks/01_benchmark_data_analysis.ipynb`
and `code/sessions/01_analyze_benchmark_data/`.

## Modalities

### 1) 2p GCaMP (Source Sub-volume)
- In-vivo 2-photon cortical z-stack (CZStack).
- Image size: 512 × 512 × ~450 planes.
- Field of view options (from `manual workflow/step_1_process_files.ipynb`):
  400 um (XY resolution 0.78 um/pixel) or 700 um (1.37 um/pixel). All current
  benchmark subjects use 400 um.
- Z resolution: ~1 um/pixel.
- Physical extent (measured from centroids, per subject): CZ cells span
  ~392–398 um (x), ~392–396 um (y), ~404–424 um (z).
- Content: ~785–1016 GCaMP+ inhibitory neurons, with 3D ROI segmentation masks.
- From pia surface; the top ~50–110 um is blank buffer (no cells). Measured
  pia-plane offsets per subject: 55–108 um.
- Sub-volume embedded within the larger HCR volume.

### 2) HCR Light-sheet (Target Volume)
- Ex-vivo HCR light-sheet imaging, matched to the in-vivo 2p volume (but
  larger).
- Raw XY resolution ≈ 0.245 um/pixel; Z resolution ≈ 1 um/pixel.
- **Cell centroids are stored at level-2 pyramid pixels** (`cell_centroids.npy`),
  where XY has been downsampled 4× from raw. This is the coordinate system the
  manual coregistration workflow operates in, using the conversion from
  `manual workflow/step_2_automatic_mapping_for_qc.ipynb`:

  ```
  scale_x = dimensions.x[0] * 4e6   # ~0.98 um per level-2 pixel
  scale_y = dimensions.y[0] * 4e6
  scale_z = dimensions.z[0] * 1e6   # ~1 um per pixel
  ```

- Level-2 effective resolution: ~0.980–0.989 um/pixel XY, ~1 um/pixel Z
  (slightly varies per subject; essentially isotropic in pixel space).
- Physical extent (level-2 centroids): ~1.8–2.3 mm XY, 0.88–1.34 mm Z.
  (The earlier "~1 × 1 × 0.5 mm" figure was based on raw-pixel volume, not the
  centroid coordinate space. The coregistration works in the centroid space.)
- Content per volume: ~39 k–127 k Rn28S-segmented cells (405 nm); 488-channel
  GFP+ subset ≈ 25 k–78 k at a 5-spot-per-cell threshold (matching the 2p
  GCaMP+ neurons).
- 3D ROI segmentation from Rn28S; 488-channel GFP quantification comes from
  one of three sources (same convention as
  `manual workflow/step_2_automatic_mapping_for_qc.ipynb`):
  1. pre-aggregated per-cell counts
     `{coreg_dir}/*_spot_488_counts.csv` (782149, 788406, 790322).
  2. raw per-spot CSV aggregated on the fly via `SEG_ID.value_counts()` and
     joined with `cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle` for volume/density
     (767018 and any subject that lacks the pre-aggregated file).
  3. per-cell mean intensity `data/cell_data_mean_{subj}_R1.csv` channel 488
     (755252, 767022). No spot count is available here, only a fluorescence
     intensity.
  A GFP+ cell is defined by `counts >= gfp_min_spots` (default 5) when a spot
  count exists. The loader exposes `gfp_min_spots` as a parameter so the
  threshold can be revised from later analyses. Intensity-only subjects use
  the raw `mean` and must be thresholded by the caller.
- Top part contains blank buffer. Thickness is unknown, varies across subjects
  (measured pia offsets: 30–410 um), and is **slanted** (measured pia-plane
  tilt: 1.2°–11.6°, with 782149 the most tilted).

## Benchmark-subject statistics

Measured per-subject values (all coordinates converted to physical microns).

GFP+ counts below use the default 5-spot threshold where applicable.

| subject | CZ cells | HCR total | HCR GFP+ (source, threshold)          | coreg matched | match rate |
|---------|---------:|----------:|---------------------------------------|--------------:|-----------:|
| 755252  | 835      | 84 233    | 77 785 (intensity_r1, no threshold)   | 639           | 76.5%      |
| 767018  | 785      | 108 506   | 58 959 (aggregated_spots_csv, ≥5)     | 273           | 34.8%      |
| 767022  | 926      | 76 336    | 72 213 (intensity_r1, no threshold)   | 793           | 85.6%      |
| 782149  | 894      | 39 291    | 25 251 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 303           | 33.9%      |
| 788406  | 932      | 127 275   | 69 680 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 787           | 84.4%      |
| 790322  | 1016     | 106 379   | 72 532 (spot_counts_csv, ≥5)          | 778           | 76.6%      |

Notes:
- 767018 lacks the pre-aggregated `spot_488_counts.csv` but has the raw
  `image_spot_detection/channel_488_spots/spots.csv` (4.86 M puncta). The
  loader aggregates it per `SEG_ID` at load time. At a 5-spot threshold,
  76 607 cells with ≥1 spot reduce to 58 959 GFP+.
- For 755252/767022 the "GFP+" figure is every HCR cell with a non-zero 488
  intensity measurement (no spot threshold available). A downstream intensity
  threshold is required to obtain a comparable GFP+ count.

## Key relationship between 2p and HCR

- CZ sub-volume is localized inside HCR.
- **XY rotation is ~180°** around the z-axis (measured: angles −177° to +180°,
  always near 180° in absolute value; this is not a flip).
- Both start from the cortex surface (pia at top, deep cortex at bottom).
- ROI segmentations have errors in both modalities, so ROI-to-ROI is not 1:1.

## Transform characterisation

Measured from active landmarks (500–800 per subject, final QC'd iteration).
An anisotropic similarity is fit (rotation + diagonal scale + translation).

### Anisotropic expansion (CZ in-vivo → HCR ex-vivo, physical microns)

| subject | XY expansion (mean) | Z expansion | XY:Z anisotropy |
|---------|--------------------:|------------:|----------------:|
| 755252  | 1.64×               | 2.13×       | 0.77            |
| 767018  | 1.70×               | 3.58×       | 0.47            |
| 767022  | 1.81×               | 2.49×       | 0.73            |
| 782149  | 1.92×               | 2.93×       | 0.66            |
| 788406  | 1.78×               | 2.82×       | 0.63            |
| 790322  | 1.77×               | 3.04×       | 0.58            |

Expansion is consistently anisotropic: XY expands ~1.6–1.9×, Z expands ~2.1–3.6×
(Z is ~1.5× more expanded than XY). A single isotropic scale cannot align the
modalities.

### Residual deformation after anisotropic affine (landmark RMS, microns)

| subject | residual RMS | residual max |
|---------|-------------:|-------------:|
| 755252  | 27           | 83           |
| 767018  | 25           | 88           |
| 767022  | 16           | 53           |
| 782149  | 29           | 65           |
| 788406  | 43           | 114          |
| 790322  | 22           | 91           |

After the affine fit, residual magnitudes reach ~25–45 um RMS, up to ~110 um
max. This is the scale of the nonrigid deformation the algorithm must handle
after affine alignment.

### Anisotropy introduced by coordinate systems

In addition to the physical expansion, the pixel coordinate systems themselves
are slightly anisotropic: CZ is 0.78 um/px XY vs 1 um/px Z, and HCR is ~0.988
um/px XY vs 1 um/px Z. When registering in pixel space, the RBF absorbs both
the physical expansion and this pixel-space anisotropy.

## Pia surface and depth-from-surface coordinate

Two surface-fitting methods were evaluated:

1. **Image-based (primary)**: for each (y, x) column of the 3D image, find
   the first z at which intensity exceeds a per-column relative threshold
   `col_bg + 0.05 × (col_top − col_bg)` (`col_bg = 10-pct`,
   `col_top = 95-pct`). The low 5 % margin is essential — larger margins
   (25 % initially tried) skip the sparse outer tissue layer and anchor the
   plane in dense cortex. Require a ≥ 15 um contiguous above-threshold
   segment and reject columns whose dynamic range is below a minimum.
   IRLS-Huber plane fit with tile-median outlier rejection.

   **For HCR** we use a **combined-channel volume**: load every available
   channel (405, 488, 561 or 514, 594), normalise each by its own
   background/percentile, and sum. Autofluorescence aligns across channels
   exactly at the tissue–buffer transition, producing a crisper boundary
   than any single channel. 488 alone is too sparse (~100–200 valid columns
   vs ~30 000); 405 alone works but can overshoot into dense cortex.
   Level 4 of the zarr pyramid (~4 um / voxel) is used.

2. **Hybrid (default for HCR)**: Uses ROI segmentation data to constrain the
   image-based search window. Steps:
   (a) Density filter: keep only ROIs with ≥6 neighbors within 30 um (removes
       isolated out-of-tissue false positives).
   (b) Fit a plane to the minimum-z ROI per (x, y) tile (coarse prior
       `z_prior(x, y)`).
   (c) In the image-based search, restrict each column to z ∈
       [z_prior − 100 um, z_prior + 100 um] before finding the first-crossing.
   (d) Fit the final plane to the constrained first-crossing map.
   This exploits the ROI-based coarse localization as a guard against the
   image-based search getting confused by noise far from the tissue boundary.
   **It is the current default in `analyze_subject()`.**

3. **Centroid-based (fallback)**: tile-top quantile of ROI centroids + IRLS-
   Huber plane fit. Kept for diagnostic comparison. It systematically
   underestimates the pia by 50–200 um because the topmost segmented ROIs
   already sit below the real tissue boundary.

Fit quality is judged by the **fraction-above-pia**: share of cells whose
centroid lies > 5 um above the estimated pia. It should be small (<~10 %);
residual positives are out-of-tissue segmentation false positives or a thin
layer of pial/meningeal cells.

### Hybrid fits (current default for HCR)

| subject | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
|---------|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
| 755252  | 259        | 7.8          | 37             | 4.0 %          |
| 767018  | 130        | 6.6          | 37             | 2.5 %          |
| 767022  | 267        | 3.1          | 69             | 4.4 %          |
| 782149  | 6          | 11.4         | 35             | 5.7 %          |
| 788406  | 148        | 2.9          | 57             | 5.8 %          |
| 790322  | 236        | 3.7          | 47             | 8.2 %          |

Hybrid is better than image-only for 767018 (10.8% → 2.5%) and 782149
(8.2% → 5.7%); slightly worse for 755252 and 790322 (< 2 pp regression).
The constrained search eliminates the case where the image-based fitter
wanders into a noise peak far above the tissue.

### Image-based fits (for comparison)

| subject | CZ c (um) | CZ tilt (°) | CZ rough (um) | CZ frac-above | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
|---------|---------:|------------:|--------------:|--------------:|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
| 755252  | 57       | 3.1         | 12            | 1.0 %         | 202        | 9.3          | 39             | 2.1 %          |
| 767018  | 46       | 1.9         | 4             | 0.0 %         | 238        | 6.5          | 106            | 10.8 %         |
| 767022  | 52       | 0.7         | 7             | 0.3 %         | 290        | 3.9          | 87             | 4.6 %          |
| 782149  | 51       | 2.0         | 10            | 3.8 %         | 18         | 11.1         | 45             | 8.2 %          |
| 788406  | 57       | 2.9         | 4             | 0.0 %         | 135        | 2.8          | 93             | 5.2 %          |
| 790322  | 50       | 1.0         | 3             | 0.0 %         | 169        | 3.2          | 89             | 6.6 %          |

### Centroid-based fits (for comparison)

| subject | HCR c (um) | HCR tilt (°) | HCR rough (um) | HCR frac-above |
|---------|-----------:|-------------:|---------------:|---------------:|
| 755252  | 390        | 6.9          | 96             | 9.0 %          |
| 767018  | 315        | 5.0          | 124            | 11.2 %         |
| 767022  | 409        | 3.0          | 84             | 8.4 %          |
| 782149  | 30         | 11.6         | 94             | 15.7 %         |
| 788406  | 248        | 1.2          | 98             | 7.6 %          |
| 790322  | 256        | 4.0          | 85             | 8.5 %          |

Observations:
- CZ pia offset is tight (46–57 um), tilt ≤ 3°, roughness ≤ 12 um — a
  plane fit on the 3D image is essentially perfect.
- HCR pia offset varies widely (18–290 um, the "slanted" remark is
  reflected in tilts up to ~11°). 782149 is the thinnest section — its
  small offset (c=18) means the zarr starts essentially at the pia.
- `fraction-above-pia` is 0–11 % for image-based fits, ~8–16 % for
  centroid-based fits. The remaining positives are mostly out-of-tissue
  segmentation false positives and a few meningeal cells just above the
  cortex proper.
- Depth-from-surface (`depth = z − z_surface(x, y)`) is a robust axial
  coordinate that removes buffer-thickness and pia-tilt confounds.

## Candidate features for automatic coregistration

Measured on all 6 subjects:

- **Cell nearest-neighbor distances** (mean 1-NN, um):
  - CZ: 21.5–24.6; HCR all: 17.1–21.1; HCR GFP+: 18.6–25.0.
  - Characterises packing density; informs k-NN radii for matching.
- **Depth density profiles**: cells/20-um bin vs depth from pia, for CZ, HCR
  total, and HCR GFP+. Cross-correlation (after Z-expansion rescaling) provides
  a 1D axial localization cue. Peaks/troughs in density reflect cortical layer
  structure and can be used as landmarks.
- **Pia surface normal**: an initial-alignment cue independent of cell
  positions; the CZ and HCR surface normals should be aligned after the ~180°
  rotation.
- **GFP+ local density peaks**: candidate constellations for the manual
  workflow's "unique clustered pattern" step.
- **ROI volumes / morphology** (from HCR `cell_body_segmentation/metrics.pickle`
  and CZ segmentation masks): available for per-cell matching confidence.

## Coordinate-system conventions (summary)

| source | column order | units | notes |
|--------|--------------|-------|-------|
| CZ centroid CSV | `[czstack_cell_id, czstack_z, czstack_y, czstack_x]` | pixels (0.78 um XY, 1 um Z) | |
| HCR centroid NPY | `[z, y, x, id]` | pixels (HCR level-2 ~0.988 um XY, 1 um Z) | |
| Landmark CSV | `[ids, active, cz_x, cz_y, cz_z, hcr_x, hcr_y, hcr_z]` | pixels in each modality's native space | no header |
| `spot_488_counts.csv` | `[hcr_id, counts, volume, global_bbox, density]` | 488 GFP feature | only for 782149/788406/790322 |
| `cell_data_mean_*_R1.csv` | channel-indexed intensity means | raw intensity | GFP+ proxy for 755252/767022 |
| Coreg table | `[czstack_id, hcr_id]` | — | final mapping |

## Data-format variations across subjects

- **767018**: uses an older processing pipeline. No `fused_ng.json` (use the
  fallback resolution). HCR cell centroids are in
  `767018_HCR_cell_centroids.csv` in addition to
  `cell_body_segmentation/cell_centroids.npy`. GFP+ is derived by aggregating
  the raw `image_spot_detection/channel_488_spots/spots.csv` on `SEG_ID` at
  load time (no pre-aggregated `spot_488_counts.csv`).
- **755252, 767022**: no `spot_488_counts.csv`. Use
  `/root/capsule/data/cell_data_mean_{subject}_R1.csv` with channel 488 mean
  intensity as GFP+ proxy. Thresholding is required to obtain a true GFP+ set.
- **782149**: thinner HCR section (878 um Z vs 1160–1329 um for others);
  strongest pia tilt (~12°); lowest match rate (~34%).
- **Landmark file naming**: older subjects (755252, 767018, 767022) use a
  `_reordered_qced` suffix; newer ones use `_qced`. Benchmark loaders must
  handle both.

## Testing priorities

- Primary: **788406** and **790322** (highest-quality data, spot-based GFP+).

content.text:
Read the relevant docs (10 Grand Plan v3, 04 manual, 11 WIP, 01 Data, 02 Goals, 03 Problem) and the surface-registration cache. (`md_for_claude/` doesn't exist — actual docs live at `code/docs/`.)

Before implementing, I need a quick alignment, because "feature extraction from the 488 HCR volume inside the overlap" can mean three different things and the architecture differs:

**Current state (what's already built).**
- Overlap is defined by `surface_registration_v2.get_surface_registration(s)` → `crop_bbox = [y0, y1, x0, x1]` in HCR **level-4 (~4 µm/px) XY**, plus a Z slab from the locked pia + Stage B `sz` × CZ z-extent.
- Per-HCR-ROI features from 488 (mean / p10/50/90, core/shell, neighbour density, GFP+ score) are already shipped in `roi_quality_v5d.py`. So "per-ROI 488 features" is largely solved.
- What is **not** built: a volumetric / image-side 488 feature stack inside the overlap, independent of the existing ROI table.

**What "feature extraction protocol" probably means here — three plausible targets:**
1. **Voxel/patch feature volumes** (Frangi/vesselness, LoG blob, gradient magnitude, local NCC against CZ) — feeds Track-B per-pair NCC (§4.2) and possibly subgoal 4.5 TPS expansion.
2. **Anchor/landmark descriptors** — sparse keypoints (DoG/Harris in 3D) with local 488 descriptors for landmark matching (revives P15 FPFH-style work in §4.1).
